segments: Microsoft 365 Commercial products and cloud services
Microsoft 365 Consumer products and cloud services

dependencies: Limited suppliers for certain device and datacenter components
Datacenters depend on availability of permitted and buildable land, predictable energy, networking supplies, and servers including GPUs
Very few qualified suppliers for certain components
risks: Extended or unforeseen disruptions at suppliers could impact ability to operate datacenters and manufacture devices
Capacity constraints from competitors using same suppliers
Supply chain disruptions in Asia and other geographies
Component shortages, excess or obsolete inventory
Constraints in supply chain of device components

regulatory_risks: Competition laws and new market regulation in EU, UK, US, China
EU Digital Markets Act compliance requirements
EU Digital Services Act compliance requirements
Data protection and privacy regulations globally
Online content and digital safety regulations
AI regulatory requirements (model development, deployment, frontier model safety, transparency, content provenance)
US Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and anti-corruption laws
US import controls on information and communication technologies
Greenhouse gas emissions and energy usage caps
Income tax regulations including OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax
IRS transfer pricing audits and disputes

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions in US, EU, and other countries prohibit business with restricted entities or countries
US export controls restrict offering products/services to certain entities in specified countries
Periods of intense diplomatic or armed conflict could result in new and rapidly evolving sanctions and trade restrictions
Negative impacts to regional trade ecosystems
Geopolitical instabilities and changing US Administration priorities create unpredictable trade landscape
